reforms.description: The recently activated R&D Public Policy Network aims to enhance coordination of regional and national innovation policies, addressing the fragmentation in innovation support that complicates navigation for firms. Efforts will continue to improve national and regional cooperation in the design and implementation of innovation policies to avoid duplication and enhance effectiveness. The reactivation of the R&D Public Policy Network (REDIDI) aims to create synergies in the implementation of national and regional smart specialisation strategies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing coordination and cooperation in innovation policies is expected to improve the effectiveness of innovation support, thereby fostering a more conducive environment for firms to innovate.
reforms.status_evidence: The R&D Public Policy Network has been activated and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform significantly enhances the coordination of innovation policies, which is crucial for improving the overall innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: Innovation support is fragmented, which makes it harder for firms to navigate the system and can lead to duplication. Give the recently activated R&D Public Policy Network a strong mandate to further increase coordination of regional and national innovation policies.

reforms.description: The government aims to strengthen the ex-post evaluation framework of innovation support and consider increasing performance-based funding to enhance the effectiveness of innovation funding. The creation of the Ministry of Science, Innovation and Universities aims to integrate science, innovation, and higher education policies to foster a more cohesive approach to innovation. Additionally, the new National Plan for Science, Technology and Innovation 2017-20 aims to consolidate information on all support schemes from different institutions, enhancing transparency and accessibility for innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening evaluation frameworks and integrating policies is expected to enhance the effectiveness and accessibility of innovation funding, thereby supporting innovation performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry has been created and the National Plan is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of the Ministry and the National Plan represents a significant structural change in how innovation is supported and managed in Spain.
reforms.source_quote: Innovation funding is often not tied to ex-post evaluation. Strengthen the ex-post evaluation framework of innovation support and consider increasing performance based funding.

reforms.description: The new Spanish government plans to promote digitalisation and internationalisation through lower administrative barriers and new financing forms, aiming to enhance the innovation ecosystem. Additionally, the reform aims to streamline the R&D tax credit system and improve awareness of how to apply for these credits, which is expected to enhance private sector investment in R&D. The government is focusing on enhancing innovation performance by addressing low gross domestic expenditures on R&D and encouraging business R&D investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Promoting digitalisation and streamlining R&D tax credits are expected to significantly boost private sector investment in R&D and overall innovation performance.
reforms.status_evidence: The plans have been outlined by the new government but are yet to be fully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a major shift in how the government supports innovation and R&D investment, particularly in the context of digitalisation.
reforms.source_quote: Enhancing innovation performance... gross domestic expenditures on R&D relative to GDP is low in Spain at around half the OECD average.

reforms.description: The Public Procurement Law significantly improves the public procurement system in Spain by simplifying procedures for small contracts and introducing an innovation partnership procedure aimed at developing new innovative products. In 2018, Spain extended its contract with the European Investment Fund to increase public and private funding for innovative firms at the seed and early start-up phases, raising the amount by 30%. Additionally, training vouchers for the unemployed were approved to fund training programs of their choice, aiming to improve the matching between participants and training centres. The government is also developing a new Digital Strategy for Smart Spain to ensure that the availability of digital skills complements the needs of firms, especially at the regional level.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving public procurement processes and increasing funding for innovative firms, along with enhancing training opportunities, are expected to support the growth of the startup ecosystem and improve workforce skills.
reforms.status_evidence: The Public Procurement Law has been enacted, and funding and training initiatives are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, these reforms are more incremental in nature compared to the major structural changes in other groups.
reforms.source_quote: The government is currently working on the new Digital Strategy for Smart Spain, which should ensure complementarities between the availability of skills and the needs of firms.
